nuggets.headline: Jamee Cornelia is actively working against her instinct to do everything herself
nuggets.text: While she's a multi-disciplinary artist who can handle most creative tasks alone, she has made it a specific goal to collaborate more. In a 2026 interview, she admitted, "I'm the type of person that does so much by myself that sometimes I'll forget to reach out because I nine times out of 10 can do it." She's now making a conscious effort to link up with friends on music, art, and social media content.

source.title: The Midnight Drive with Damien Jaxxyn: Interview with Jamee ...
source.publisher: WEFT 90.1 FM, Community Radio for East Central Illinois
source.quoteSnippet: I'm the type of person that does so much by myself that sometimes I'll forget to reach out because I nine times out of 10 can do it. Um, and so I've been this year I've been like making it a goal to like collaborate.

nuggets.headline: Jamee Cornelia's creative identity flourished after moving from her North Carolina hometown
nuggets.text: Cornelia identifies as a Queer Black Woman and has spoken about the challenges of starting out in a male-dominated field in the South. She found that the very aspects of her identity that made people treat her poorly in her hometown of Fuquay Varina, NC, are what allowed her to flourish in East Atlanta. Her advice to other artists is to travel often to find an environment where they are valued.

source.title: Conversations with the Inspiring Jamee Cornelia
source.publisher: Voyage ATL Magazine | ATL City Guide
source.quoteSnippet: Well, the biggest bump for me is that I am a Queer Black Woman in a male-dominated field in the South... the same things that made me feel lesser than... I am FLOURISHING for in East Atlanta.

nuggets.imageCaption: Jamee Cornelia, whose creative career took off after relocating to Atlanta.

nuggets.headline: If you want to hear Jamee Cornelia's creative circle, listen to June Jizzel
nuggets.text: In an interview, Jamee Cornelia gave a shout-out to her friend June Jizzel, a creative she linked up with in LA who was doing radio work with Future Surf Radio. Jizzel makes his own music and remixes, blending anime aesthetics with hip-hop beats. He represents the network of collaborators Cornelia has been intentionally building around herself.

source.title: The Midnight Drive with Damien Jaxxyn: Interview with Jamee ...
source.publisher: WEFT 90.1 FM, Community Radio for East Central Illinois
source.quoteSnippet: Oh, that's funny. Shout out June Jizzel. Yeah, that was funny. That's the homie. Uh he he does like a radio stuff with uh well he did radio stuff with Future Surf Radio and that's my homie.

nuggets.imageCaption: June Jizzel, a collaborator and friend within Jamee Cornelia's creative network.

artistSummary: Jamee Cornelia is a multi-talented creative force from the Atlanta area, with roots in everything from punk bands and skate teams to video direction and running an indie label. Her work is defined by a fierce DIY ethos and a brutally honest perspective, blending genres with a raw, personable energy.
